structural_claim: Physical systems spontaneously break continuous symmetries to form discrete, ordered structures through critical transitions governed by dimensionless parameters

supporting_results.structural_conclusion: Below a critical temperature, a continuous ensemble of atoms undergoes symmetry breaking to form a discrete macroscopic quantum state
Above a critical Rayleigh number, continuous fluid undergoes symmetry breaking to form discrete convection cells
Rotating flows undergo hierarchical symmetry-breaking transitions at critical Reynolds numbers, forming discrete vortex structures
